summary_one_line: Profile connection-space signature field at offset 0x10 must equal the FOURCC 'Lab ' so InPCS hits cmsSigLabData.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace/PCS signature in the profile header (offset 0x10 in these seeds) must be the FOURCC 'Lab ' = 0x4c 0x61 0x62 0x20, so InPCS in AddConversion's switch equals cmsSigLabData and the true side at line 452 is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20) at offset 0x10-0x13, matching the cmsSigLabData switch constant; the per-role overlay then enters the Lab-input arm (lines 454-478) marked [W].
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds all carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offset 0x10, an RGB/non-PCS device colorspace; InPCS therefore never equals cmsSigLabData and the case-452 true side is never taken (loser hitcount on branch = 0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the switch comparison against the cmsSigLabData literal, records the constant 'Lab ' in the CmpLogObserver buffer, and substitutes it into the input at the offset where the prior operand ('RGB ' / other signature) appeared; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot directly plant the exact 4-byte magic, so they stall on the false side.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the switch case selecting Lab-input PCS handling in AddConversion.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: case cmsSigLabData: // Input profile operates in Lab

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique only.
The second decisive pair (vpc>vp) is also an I2S delta.
Winner-resolving seeds contain the ASCII 'Lab ' signature at offset 0x10.
Loser-blocking seeds carry the 'RGB ' signature at offset 0x10 instead of 'Lab '.
The byte diff localizes the discriminating bytes to offsets 0x10-0x12 where winners are L/Lab-leaning and losers are uniformly R/G/B.
The loser never takes the true side of the blocker (hitcount 0), while the winner takes it many times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=165  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot directly plant the operand.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2088 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0b8368b936d0cbb9` and observed one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 14 (ancestor 5441efb6193a1a03); also ran `--fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 044d3fc2a2e1afbd` and observed one dash row at depth 17 (same ancestor). Both decisive winners' chains contain the I2S-floor signal.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A naive or value_profile trial reaching the case-452 true side at comparable rate without ever placing the exact 'Lab ' FOURCC at the PCS-signature offset (i.e., resolving the branch by some byte pattern other than the cmsSigLabData literal), which would show the divergence is not driven by I2S magic-value substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact input offset that maps to InPCS is inferred from the seed-byte 'Lab '/'RGB ' alignment at 0x10 rather than from explicit source-to-byte tracing of the profile-header parse.
